news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) Gets Updated 3.3% Ownership Filing From Millennium Group
news.summary: Millennium Management and affiliated funds filed an amended Schedule 13G/A disclosing a reduced 3.3% passive stake in Tenax Therapeutics, down from the 5.1% stake reported in May 2026. The trim comes ahead of the Phase 3 LEVEL data readout expected in August 2026.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: Tenax granted non-qualified stock option inducement awards covering an aggregate 160,000 shares to three newly hired non-executive employees in accounting, clinical operations, and CMC/product development roles, vesting over four years. The awards were approved outside the shareholder equity plan as required under Nasdaq hiring-inducement rules.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Late-Breaking Presentation of LEVEL Clinical Trial Data at ESC Congress 2026
news.summary: Tenax will present full Phase 3 LEVEL trial results for oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session at the European Society of Cardiology Congress 2026 in Munich on August 29, with topline data now expected in August 2026 ahead of database lock and statistical analysis.

news.headline: Tenax and Orion Secure Long-Term Levosimendan Supply Deal
news.summary: Tenax and Orion Corporation signed a five-year, auto-renewing supply agreement naming Orion the primary manufacturer of oral levosimendan (TNX-103), alongside a Sixth Amendment to their 2013 license agreement extending the deadline for U.S. regulatory approval to December 31, 2035.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Added to Russell 2000 and Russell 3000E Indexes
news.summary: Tenax Therapeutics was added to the Russell 2000, Russell 3000E, and related Russell Microcap/Value benchmark indexes effective with FTSE Russell's June 2026 semi-annual reconstitution, broadening the stock's exposure to passive index-fund ownership ahead of the LEVEL data readout.

news.headline: Guggenheim Raises Tenax Therapeutics Price Target to $40; Chardan Capital Initiates Coverage at Buy
news.summary: Guggenheim reiterated its Buy rating on TENX and lifted its price target to $40 from $34, while Chardan Capital initiated coverage the same day with a Buy rating and a $27 price target, both citing the approaching Phase 3 LEVEL data readout as the key catalyst.

catalysts.label: Phase 3 LEVEL topline data readout
catalysts.detail: Tenax expects to report topline data from the registrational Phase 3 LEVEL trial of oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in PH-HFpEF in August 2026, pulled forward from prior Q3 2026 guidance.

catalysts.label: Late-breaking LEVEL data presentation at ESC Congress 2026
catalysts.detail: Full LEVEL trial results will be presented in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session at the European Society of Cardiology Congress in Munich (Aug 28-31), presented by Prof. Sanjiv Shah, MD, Northwestern University, on Saturday Aug 29.

catalysts.label: LEVEL-2 global Phase 3 enrollment completion
catalysts.detail: The second registrational Phase 3 study (LEVEL-2, ~540 patients across 100+ sites in 15 countries) is expected to complete enrollment by the end of 2027.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Tenax's next quarterly earnings report and corporate update is estimated for mid-August 2026 (Q1 2026 results were released May 12), likely alongside or shortly after the LEVEL topline data disclosure.

pulse: As of July 19, 2026, Tenax Therapeutics remains in a quiet holding pattern ahead of the pivotal readout for oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in pulmonary hypertension due to HFpEF, with topline Phase 3 LEVEL data pulled forward to August 2026 and a late-breaking full data presentation confirmed for ESC Congress in Munich on August 29. Recent housekeeping items include a trim in Millennium Management's ownership stake (down to 3.3% from 5.1%), routine inducement option grants to new hires, a long-term manufacturing supply deal with Orion that extends the US approval deadline to 2035, and inclusion in the Russell 2000/3000E indexes. Sell-side sentiment stays predominantly bullish (Piper Sandler $50, Guggenheim $40, Chardan $27 Buy initiation, consensus ~"Moderate Buy"), though at least one independent analysis has shifted to a cautious "Hold" given the binary, all-or-nothing nature of the LEVEL trial's 6-minute-walk-distance endpoint, where a miss could cut shares sharply despite cash runway into Q1 2028 ($118.8M as of March 31, 2026).
